data.title: Eurozone unemployment rate holds steady at 6.2% in May
data.description:
The unemployment rate in the Euro Area remained unchanged at 6.20 percent in May. 

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (DAX)

data.title: SoftBank’s LY and Bain Capital lift Kakaku bid to $4.1B
data.description:
• SoftBank’s (SFTBY) LY Corp (YAHOF) and Bain Capital have raised their takeover offer for Kakaku.com, valuing the Japanese price-comparison platform at about $4.1B as the bidding war with Sweden’s EQT intensifies.
  • The consortium increased its bid to 3,384 yen per share, up from 3,232 yen previously, according to the latest proposal, and it marks the latest escalation in a months-long contest for control of Kakaku.com.
  • The revised offer now sits above EQT’s competing bid of 3,000 yen per share, which had earlier received backing from Kakaku’s board.
  • LY (YAHOF) and Bain said their offer would rise to 3,500 yen per share if KDDI Corp (KDDIY), one of Kakaku.com’s largest shareholders, agreed to support the bid.
  • Kakaku operates several high-traffic digital platforms, including its price-comparison site Kakaku.com, restaurant review service Tabelog, and job listings platform Kyujin Box, making it a strategically valuable asset in Japan’s increasingly active M&A market.

data.title: US stock futures mixed as Wall Street awaits key employment data report
data.description:
Stock index futures were mixed on Thursday as investors awaited the closely watched nonfarm payrolls report, due a day early ahead of the July 4 Independence Day holiday.

Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) fell 0.38%. S&P 500 futures (SPX) edged higher by 0.02% and Dow futures (INDU) rose 0.09%.

Wall Street's attention is firmly on the June employment data due at 8:30 a.m. ET, with economists expecting the U.S. economy to have added 114,000 jobs last month.

A stronger-than-expected reading could reinforce expectations that the Federal Reserve will tighten policy later this year, with markets currently pricing in roughly an 80% chance of a September move.

Treasury yields edged higher across key benchmarks. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 0.05 basis points to 4.19%, the 10-year yield (US10Y) climbed 1.1 basis points to 4.50%, and the longer-term 30-year yield (US30Y) moved up 1.3 basis points to 4.99%.

Top gainers in premarket trading included Aon (AON) +7.52%, Cintas (CTAS) +4.43%, and KKR (KKR) +4.24%. Decliners included CrowdStrike (CRWD) -74.62%, SanDisk (SNDK) -3.85%, and Dell Technologies (DELL) -3.34%.

data.title: Embraer buys remaining 50% stake in EZ Air Interior JV
data.description:
• Embraer (EMBJ) completed the acquisition of Safran Cabin’s 50% stake in its EZ Air Interior joint venture, taking full ownership of the Mexico-based cabin interiors operation after receiving regulatory approvals.
  • The agreement includes the acquisition of the remaining 50% stake in Mexico-based EZ Air Interior, a joint venture established in 2012 between Embraer and C&D (now Safran Cabin) in Chihuahua. The facility has exclusively supported Embraer programs, producing cabin components for the E-Jet family, including luggage bins, galleys, lavatories, and floor panels.
  • Alongside the JV buyout, Embraer is also integrating selected Safran Cabin operations in Brazil tied to Embraer programs, while Safran retains its non-Embraer-related engineering and cabin services businesses.
  • The financial details of the deal were not yet disclosed.

data.title: Franklin Covey sinks after cutting revenue outlook on delivery shifts and China uncertainty
data.description:
Shares of Franklin Covey (FC) tumbled 12% in premarket trading on Thursday following an earnings miss and a cautionary outlook from management regarding ongoing geopolitical tensions impacting its international and China operations.

The management also flagged execution risk around delivery timing of contracted services and variability in the fourth-quarter close, while emphasizing cost reductions as the mitigation supporting adjusted EBITDA guidance.

Franklin Covey (FC) framed fiscal 2026 as a year when invoiced growth and deferred revenue build a base for fiscal 2027 but revised the full-year revenue range to $260M-$267M due to service-delivery timing shifts, an education state funding cut, and international/geopolitical pressures.

Regarding China, CEO Paul Walker said management was “looking at some options” and would share more after evaluating “different options” for operating in China.

Asked for the AI roadmap, Walker said Franklin Covey had launched AI offerings, expanded work with a large technology client in Q3, and planned “the next set of modules” in early fall plus “additional functionality within our AI coach.”

The stock is up 49% so far this year.

data.title: Idaho Copper prices $18M public offering at $4.85/share, commences trading on NYSE American
Europe markets in green as risk appetite lifts
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.26%. The pound climbed to $1.33, a two-week high against the dollar, as investors embraced riskier assets amid signs of progress in indirect U.S.-Iran talks

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.33%, the highest in over a week, marking the third straight session of gains.

France (CAC:IND) +0.63%. France's state budget deficit narrowed to €93.3B in January–May 2026 from €94B in the same period last year.

In other parts of Europe, Spain’s registered unemployment fell by 28.739K from the previous month to 2.291M in June 2026.

Consumer prices in Switzerland rose by 0.5% Y/Y in June.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) edged up 0.29% to €641,  supported by the region's relatively limited exposure to the technology sector. Technology stocks came under pressure, with ASML (ASML) leading the decline. In contrast, healthcare stocks advanced.

The euro rebounded to $1.14 but remained near one-year lows against the dollar and pound, pressured by softer-than-expected Eurozone inflation and dovish remarks from ECB President Christine Lagarde.

In the bond market, the yield on the US 10-year Treasury was up 2 basis points to 4.49%

UK's 10-year yield was up 4 basis points to 4.79%. 

Germany's 10-year yield was up 3 basis points to 2.96%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

data.title: German drone startup Quantum Systems secures $1.2B at $8B valuation
data.description:
Quantum Systems has raised $1.2B in a new funding round, more than doubling the German drone maker's valuation to about $8B post-money.

The round was co-led by Blackstone (BX), Noteus, Airbus (EADSF) (EADSY), and Advent. The company said that the proceeds will support international expansion, boost manufacturing capacity, and fund acquisitions.

Based in Gilching, Bavaria, Quantum Systems is among several German defense startups capitalizing on Europe's surge in military spending. The company, which develops unmanned aerial vehicles, raised capital in November at a valuation of €3B. It has supplied surveillance drones to Ukraine since Russia's full-scale invasion and has since expanded its portfolio to include unmanned land and maritime systems.

Peter Thiel is one of Quantum Systems' early investors. The billionaire entrepreneur, a co-founder of Palantir Technologies (PLTR) and a prominent supporter of U.S. President Donald Trump, has drawn attention in Germany over his investments in defense startups.

However, the company said it has not faced scrutiny from the German government over Thiel's investment. Earlier this year, Germany's Defense Ministry briefed lawmakers on Thiel's minority stake in Stark Defence, according to Bloomberg.
